pattern: Each leading digit governs a block of (n-1)! permutations, so dividing k by the factorial picks the next digit directly instead of enumerating sequences.
hints: The permutations in order fall into n groups by their first digit, and each group has exactly (n-1)! members.
Working with a zero-based rank, k // (n-1)! tells you which unused digit comes first, and the remainder is the rank within that group.
Precompute factorials and keep the unused digits in a list. Repeatedly take index = k // f, append that digit, remove it, set k %= f, and shrink the factorial - this builds the answer digit by digit.

edge_inputs.description: single element has only one permutation
first permutation is the ascending order
last permutation is the descending order
k landing exactly on a block boundary
largest n with its final permutation

stress.description: random n with a valid k drawn from 1..n!
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    import math
    n = rng.randint(1, 9)
    k = rng.randint(1, math.factorial(n))
    return [n, k]
stress.note: k is always within 1..n! per the constraint
